title: Proper error codes for content policy rejections
summary:
A quiet day fixing HTTP status codes so content policy rejections return a 422 instead of a panic-inducing 500.

We stopped throwing generic 500 errors when upstream content moderation kicks in. You'll now get a proper 422 with a `content_policy_violation` code, meaning you can actually handle these programmatically instead of regexing error strings. As a bonus, our status monitors will stop panicking about degraded models when users just ask for spicy things.
